data.body:
PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection)

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes):
- G6 fitness-for-purpose: The plan solves the NAMED problem (two parallel outbound appliers → capability drift), not a proxy. Verified against the live code: reconcile.py:74-79 maps (outbound, update) → applier._apply_outbound_update in the typed dispatch table, but the actual outbound execution path (applier.py:473-491) normalizes typed Mutations via _mutation_to_batch_dict and routes them through _apply_batch / batch_dispatch.update_one. So the typed leaf _apply_outbound_update IS dead for production outbound updates, exactly as the plan states; capabilities were re-added to the batch path one bug at a time (parent: batch_dispatch.py:387-400; comments/labels: 474-520 "Bug 87e4"; links: 522-530 "Bug 3f04"). Collapsing to one path is the correct structural fix.
- G6 mechanism (sequencing): The "verify parity BEFORE deleting the retired path" ordering is sound — it prevents silently dropping a capability on deletion.
- G6 positive rationale: The plan states a positive rationale for the chosen direction ("chosen so there is no separately-tested parallel that production skips"). PASS — no missing-rationale finding.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] The plan's What presents the consolidation DIRECTION as an open either/or ("delete the dead typed leaf OR make it the single production path and route batch through it") as if the two were roughly equivalent, but they are not — commit to one direction in the plan body and state why it fits.
  criteria: G6
  location: ticket 33d0 "What" bullet 1 ("Either delete the dead typed leaf ... or make it the single production path ...")
  evidence: The two paths have DIVERGED in load-bearing ways that make the choice consequential. The batch path pushes scalars via ACLI `jira workitem edit` and carries accreted error-handling the typed leaf lacks: the illegal-transition 400 → add_comment fallback (batch_dispatch.py:454-472) and the bug-6afc `comment_errors` in-band capture (batch_dispatch.py:511-516). The typed leaf instead pushes scalars via `client.update_issue(**fields)` (apply_outbound.py:168-170). The two allowlists also differ (_OUTBOUND_BATCH_ALLOWLIST {summary,description,assignee,priority,status} at batch_dispatch.py:383-385 vs _OUTBOUND_UPDATE_ALLOWLIST in apply_outbound.py). "Make the typed leaf production" would swap the scalar-push mechanism (ACLI→REST) and force re-homing the batch path's error handling — a much larger blast radius than "keep batch as production, delete the leaf." The ticket's own file_impact already commits to the lower-risk direction ("collapse _apply_outbound_update to delegate to the production batch path"), so the plan body's open either/or contradicts the file_impact and under-specifies the chosen approach.
  suggested_fix: Replace the either/or with the single chosen direction already implied by file_impact: "Keep the batch path (batch_dispatch.update_one) as the single production applier and delete/empty the typed leaf — production already executes the batch path and all recent capability fixes (parent 8b25, comments/labels 87e4, links 3f04, illegal-transition fallback + comment_errors 6afc) already live there; routing batch through the leaf would force an ACLI→REST scalar-push swap and re-homing of that error handling for no offsetting benefit."

- [ ] The behavioral-parity Acceptance Criterion is scoped to sub-op PRESENCE only ("applies every sub-op the retired path did") and will not detect the behavioral DIVERGENCES between the two paths, so a green parity check could still mask a behavior loss on deletion.
  criteria: G6
  location: ticket 33d0 Acceptance Criteria line 2 ("Behavioral parity is verified — the surviving path applies every sub-op the retired path did")
  evidence: Per batch_dispatch.py:454-472 and :511-516 the batch path has behaviors the typed leaf does NOT (illegal-transition 400 → add_comment fallback; comment_errors in-band capture), and the typed leaf has behaviors the batch path may not (empty-work-set WARNING, apply_outbound.py:177-179). A presence-only parity assertion (does each of fields/comments/labels/links/parent fire) cannot detect that a path mishandles a 400 transition or drops the comment-error surface. If the implementer chooses the leaf-as-production direction, these batch-only behaviors could silently disappear despite a passing parity check.
  suggested_fix: Broaden the parity AC: "before deletion, enumerate every behavior unique to the retired path (illegal-transition comment fallback, comment_errors capture, empty-work-set warning, allowlist contents) and confirm each is preserved on the surviving path or explicitly retired with rationale — not merely that the five sub-ops fire."
